customer_summary.summary: The UVZ web application currently runs on Angular 18 and Pattern Library 11.3.1. Security updates for Angular 18 stop on 21‑Nov‑2025, and the only way to keep receiving patches is to pay for a Never‑Ending‑Support contract. To stay secure, compliant and avoid additional licensing costs, the application must be upgraded now to Angular 19 together with Pattern Library 12.6.0, which are the next supported versions. If the upgrade is not performed, the system will become vulnerable to unpatched security issues after the support deadline and may eventually be forced to run on an unsupported stack.
customer_summary.workaround: Continue paying for Never‑Ending‑Support on Angular 18 (costly) or accept the security risk of running an unsupported framework.

developer_context.big_picture: UVZ is a large, browser‑based client application that provides public‑service functionality to external users (e.g., citizens, businesses). It lives in the **presentation container** of the overall system and is built with Angular, consuming the internal Design System (bnotk/ds‑ng) and a shared Pattern Library (PL). The task is to move the UI stack from Angular 18 / PL 11.3.1 to Angular 19 / PL 12.6.0 so that the product remains under active security support. This upgrade is required now because the Angular 18 LTS support window closes on 21‑Nov‑2025; without it the application would no longer receive security patches, exposing the service to vulnerabilities. The upgrade does **not** change any business logic – it only updates the technology stack and removes obsolete UI artefacts.
developer_context.scope_boundary:
IN: All presentation‑layer code (Angular modules, components, services) that depend on Angular core, Angular CLI, RxJS, the Design System (bnotk/ds‑ng), ng‑bootstrap, ng‑select, ag‑grid, and the Pattern Library. Update Node.js, TypeScript, and the listed npm packages to versions compatible with Angular 19. Verify that the vertical action bar continues to work (it is deprecated but still supported until PL 13.2.0). Remove any leftover Angular 18 or PL 11 artefacts that are not required by the new versions.
OUT: Backend Java services, domain and infrastructure layers, database schemas, non‑UI libraries, and any feature work unrelated to the UI stack (e.g., new business features, API changes). The upgrade does not include a migration to Angular 20/21.
affected_components: Frontend Container – Presentation Layer (all Angular modules & components)
Vertical Action Bar component – Presentation Layer

context_boundaries.boundary: Angular 19 requires TypeScript ≥ 5.0 and Node.js ≥ 18. The current stack uses TypeScript 4.9.5 and an unspecified Node version that is only guaranteed for Angular 18. Both TypeScript and Node must be upgraded before the Angular upgrade can succeed.

All Angular packages (@angular/*) are pinned to v18‑lts. They must be moved to the matching v19 releases. Third‑party UI libraries (ng‑bootstrap, ng‑select, ag‑grid) have their own compatibility matrices; upgrading them without checking their Angular 19 support could cause runtime errors.
source_facts: dependencies.json: ag-grid-angular ^? (not listed but implied by acceptance criteria)

context_boundaries.boundary: Pattern Library 12.6.0 introduces breaking changes and deprecates the vertical action bar only up to PL 13.2.0. The upgrade must ensure the vertical action bar still functions and that no leftover PL 11 components remain.
source_facts: Issue description: vertical action bar is deprecated but usable until PL 13.2.0

context_boundaries.boundary: The project uses Karma 6.4.3 and Webpack 5.80.0 for unit tests and bundling. Angular 19 may require updates to the test runner configuration and Webpack loaders. Tests must be re‑run after the upgrade to catch breaking changes.

The build pipeline is based on Gradle 8.2.1 for the backend and Angular CLI for the frontend. Angular 19 may need a newer CLI version; the CI/CD scripts that invoke the CLI must be verified for compatibility.

developer_context.architecture_notes: The UVZ system consists of 5 containers (e.g., Backend, Frontend, Integration, Batch, Infrastructure). The Angular application lives in the **Frontend container** and occupies the **presentation layer** (≈287 components). Within this container, UI components are built on top of the internal Design System library **bnotk/ds‑ng** and consume the shared **Pattern Library**. The vertical action bar is a UI component that sits at the top‑level layout and interacts with routing services. Upgrading Angular and the Pattern Library will ripple through all presentation components, the Design System, and any shared UI utilities (ng‑bootstrap, ng‑select, ag‑grid). Downstream, the presentation layer communicates with the application layer via HTTP services; those contracts remain unchanged, so only the UI stack is affected. The developer's work area is therefore: Frontend container → Presentation layer → all Angular modules/components → neighboring libraries (ds‑ng, PL, third‑party UI libs).

anticipated_questions.question: Which versions of Node.js and TypeScript are required for Angular 19?
anticipated_questions.answer: Angular 19 officially supports Node.js ≥ 18 and TypeScript ≥ 5.0. The current project uses TypeScript 4.9.5, so both TypeScript and the Node runtime must be upgraded before the Angular upgrade can be applied.

anticipated_questions.question: Do the third‑party UI libraries (ng‑bootstrap, ng‑select, ag‑grid) have Angular 19 compatible releases?
anticipated_questions.answer: Each of those libraries publishes a compatibility matrix. The upgrade task must verify that the versions listed in the acceptance criteria have a release that supports Angular 19; if not, the library version must be bumped to the latest compatible release.

anticipated_questions.question: Will the existing unit and e2e tests still run after the upgrade?
anticipated_questions.answer: Tests are currently executed with Karma 6.4.3 and Playwright 1.44.1. Angular 19 may require updates to the Karma configuration and possibly to Webpack loaders. After the upgrade, the full test suite should be executed to catch any breaking changes.

anticipated_questions.question: Is the vertical action bar still usable after moving to Pattern Library 12.6.0?
anticipated_questions.answer: Yes, the vertical action bar is deprecated but remains supported up to PL 13.2.0. The upgrade must ensure that no PL 11 artefacts are left behind and that the component still renders correctly with PL 12.6.0.

anticipated_questions.question: Do we need to touch any backend (Java) code for this upgrade?
anticipated_questions.answer: No. The upgrade is confined to the frontend container (presentation layer). Backend services, domain logic, and data‑access components remain unchanged.
